Yummy.name: Yummy
Yummy.playing_style: "Yummy" is a Synchro archetype whose Level 2 Synchro Monsters can be Synchro Summoned using Link-1 Link Monsters as material by treating them as Level 1 Tuners. Their archetypal Link Monster, "Yummy★Snatchy", can use the Synchro Monsters as interaction by triggering Synchro Summons on the opponent's turn, allowing itself to be used as Synchro Material. The Synchro Monsters can then be returned to the Extra Deck when the opponent activates a card or effect to Special Summon "Yummy" monsters from

Vanquish Soul.name: Vanquish Soul
Vanquish Soul.playing_style: "Vanquish Soul" revolves around the shared ability to, as a Quick Effect, reveal monsters from the hand in order to activate a variety of effects depending on the revealed monster's Attribute. The deck generally plays a control/midrange strategy, aiming to generate card advantage on both players' turns to outgrind the opponent.

extra: Number C105: Battlin' Boxer Comet Cestus

Maliss.name: Maliss
Maliss.playing_style: "Maliss" revolves around using self-banishing in order to swarm the field with materials for Link Summons; most of its monsters can Special Summon themselves upon being banished. In conjunction with the Deck's Link-only Extra Deck lock, a "Maliss" Deck is typically geared towards a Link Monster beatdown strategy.

main: Lady Labrynth of the Silver Castle

Branded.name: Branded
Branded.playing_style: "Branded" is an archetype of primarily Spell and Trap Cards that support various cards related to "Fallen of Albaz" and Fusion Summoning. It also directly supports the "Despia" and "Bystial" archetypes:

Mitsurugi.name: Mitsurugi
Mitsurugi.playing_style: The "Mitsurugi" archetype is a Ritual archetype that revolves around its main Level 8 Ritual Monsters. Each monster in the archetype has an effect that activates in the Graveyard when they are Tributed, recovering card advantage and board presence when they are used as material for Ritual Summons or when they are Tributed by their own effects. The goal of the archetype is to end on "Ame no Murakumo no Mitsurugi" and "Futsu no Mitama no Mitsurugi", who together make the archetype difficult to bre

main: Manju of the Ten Thousand Hands

Dragonmaid.name: Dragonmaid
Dragonmaid.playing_style: The "Dragonmaid" archetype revolves around swarming its low-Level members and using their on-summon effects to amass resources during either the Standby or a Main Phase, switching them out for their dragon forms during the Battle Phase, and then switching back into the humanoid forms afterwards.

extra: Trishula, the Dragon of Icy Imprisonment

War Rock.name: War Rock
War Rock.playing_style: The "War Rock" archetype focuses on an aggressive playstyle centered around spamming the boards with monsters, battling opponent's monsters, and giving boosts of ATK to its members until the end of the opponent's turn after successfully activating their effects, allowing them to build up surprisingly high ATK in a short space of time. This is facilitated by the Field Spell, "War Rock Mountain", which can search a "War Rock" monster on activation and Special Summon a "War Rock" monster from the h

extra: Chevreuil, Hunting Scout of the Deep Forest
Faisan, Hunting Scout of the Deep Forest

Magikey.name: Magikey
Magikey.playing_style: "Magikey" focuses on supporting Normal Monsters of various Attributes (mainly "Clavkiys, the Magikey Skyblaster") and using them to Summon various Extra Deck monsters and Ritual Monsters, which then recycle "Clavkiys". They gain extra effects by matching the Attributes of the Normal Monsters in the GY with those of the opponent's monsters.

Batteryman.name: Batteryman
Batteryman.playing_style: "Batteryman" Decks are generally played as OTK Decks, focusing on breaking the opponent's board and dealing enough damage to win. Other variants, such as Batteryman "Hunder", also exist, though.

extra: Chaos Ruler, the Chaotic Magical Dragon

Elemental HERO.name: Elemental HERO
Elemental HERO.playing_style: The "Elemental HERO" archetype has many Main Deck monsters and Extra Deck Fusion Monsters, each with their own varied effects. Historically, the archetype can be built to focus on their Main Deck monsters or their Fusion Monsters; currently, the Main Deck "Elemental HERO" monsters are too weak in contrast to the general power level of the game, and later "Elemental HERO" support leaned into their Fusion Summoning playstyle more favorably.

HERO.name: HERO
HERO.playing_style: Due to the "HERO" archetype being generally divided across a few sub-archetypes, the overall playstyle of the "HERO" archetype is objectively varied although common traits between the sub-archetypes show that the "HERO" archetype is predominantly a Fusion-based playstyle; they focus on swarming the field by Summoning "HERO" monsters, as well as searching "HERO" monsters and/or their "Fusion"/"Polymerization" Spell Cards needed to Special Summon their Fusion Monsters (usually by a Fusion Summon).

extra: Elemental HERO Flame Wingman - Infernal Rage

Magician.name: Magician
Magician.playing_style: As a Pendulum archetype, the "Magician" Pendulum Monsters aim to search their various members to set up Pendulum Summons. The most notable of these is "Pendulum Call" which also protects "Magician" cards in the Pendulum Zones from being destroyed. Alternatively, "Wisdom-Eye Magician" can be used instead "Oafdragon Magician" is used as a Pendulum Scale to recovers "Wisdom-Eye" and be Special Summoned to recover a target for the "Discard 1 Pendulum Monster" effects mentioned above. Because of the

Fiendsmith.name: Fiendsmith
Fiendsmith.playing_style: The archetype is largely focused around its key Main Deck monster, "Fiendsmith Engraver", and its multiple Fusion Monster upgrades. Common themes of the Deck include recursion (shuffling from GY into the Deck/summoning from the GY) and Link Monsters equipping themselves to LIGHT Fiends whilst providing additional effects.

Vision HERO.name: Vision HERO
Vision HERO.playing_style:
The archetype's playstyle involves using the Level 3 "Vision HERO" monsters' effects to be placed in the Spell & Trap Zone as Continuous Traps (a.k.a. "vision" effects) to swarm the field and gain advantage, and summon the archetype's boss monsters via Tribute Summon or Fusion Summon.
The Level 3 "Vision HERO" monsters share the following three only once per turn effects:

main: The Phantom Knights of Ancient Cloak
The Phantom Knights of Silent Boots

Swordsoul.name: Swordsoul
Swordsoul.playing_style: The "Swordsoul" archetype is focused on Synchro Summoning. Instead of having archetypal Tuner monsters, "Swordsoul" cards instead have effects that Special Summon a Level 4 "Swordsoul Token" Tuner monster, which greatly increases the Deck's consistency compared to other Synchro Decks as there is no longer a need to search for Tuners. All the effects that Special Summon the "Swordsoul Token" apply the restriction that the player cannot Special Summon monsters from the Extra Deck except Synchro Mo

Salamangreat.name: Salamangreat
Salamangreat.playing_style: The "Salamangreat" archetype focuses around swarming the field with monsters to set up for Link Summons, and setting up their Graveyard with a large amount of easily recoverable resources for an OTK push on later turns. The Deck's Link Monsters have effects that they can only use when Link Summoned using a monster with the same name as Link Material (known as a Reincarnation Link Summon in the anime); the effect of the Field Spell "Salamangreat Sanctuary" allows "Salamangreat" Link Monsters to b

The.name: The
The.playing_style: The Series generally supports the Dragon Type, with a large focus on using Normal Monsters, recycling and reviving them at huge frequency to power them up or use them as Fusion Material. "The Wyvern" can specifically support many Dragon Type-based Rush Duel themes by retrieving crucial Normal Monsters, such as "The Dragon" and the materials of "Full Moon Dragon Umbralancer F" for Fusion Dragon Decks, or "Sportsdragon Keeper" for the Sportsdragon series, while The Series' boss Fusion Monster "The

Tearlaments.name: Tearlaments
Tearlaments.playing_style: The "Tearlaments" archetype is centered on milling cards from the player's Deck, in order to perform Fusion Summons with the Trigger Effect activated when a Main Deck "Tearlaments" monster is sent to the GY. The Deck has a notable secondary focus on Fusion Summoning during the opponent's turn to trigger additional beneficial Trigger Effects. As an archetype in the lore of "Visas Starfrost", certain "Tearlaments" cards also interact with "Visas Starfrost".

Favorite.name: Favorite
Favorite.playing_style: "Favorite" cards support the "HERO" ("Elemental HERO" in particular) and "Yubel" archetypes. For "HERO" support, "Favorite Hero" is an Equip Spell Card can search Field Spell Cards and protect Level 5 or higher "HERO" monsters from being targeted by the opponent's card effects while a Field Spell is present and gives the equipped monster an ATK boost (equal to its DEF), and lets the equipped monster attack again in a row, while "Favorite Contact" is a Normal Trap Card can Summon a Fusion Monster

extra: Elemental HERO Flame Wingman - Infernal Rage

Plunder Patroll.name: Plunder Patroll
Plunder Patroll.playing_style: The Plunder Patroll theme's playstyle revolves around summoning archetypal Extra Deck monsters for free during the opponent's turn based on the Attribute of the monsters the opponent controls or are in their GY. Currently, there are 5 boss monsters; a FIRE Synchro Monster, a DARK Xyz Monster, a LIGHT Fusion Monster, a EARTH Synchro Monster and a WATER Link Monster. By doing so, they can equip themselves to the cards and make the discard effects of "Plunder Patrollship Brann" and "Plunder Patroll

extra: D/D/D/D Great Dimension King Arc Crisis

Tenpai Dragon.name: Tenpai Dragon
Tenpai Dragon.playing_style: "Tenpai Dragon" is a blind going second Deck that involves around swarming their Main Deck monsters onto the field, then repeatedly Synchro Summoning "Sangen" Synchro Monsters and/or "Trident Dragion" in the Battle Phase, attacking repeatedly with these monsters until the opponent is defeated. "Trident Dragion" and both "Sangen" Synchros have effects that can destroy your own cards, allowing you to trigger the Field Spell "Sangen Summoning"'s ATK doubling effect, or send "Sangenpai Transcendent

main: Aiza the Dragoness of Deranged Devotion

Windwitch.name: Windwitch
Windwitch.playing_style: The "Windwitch" monsters focus on freely swarming the field for Synchro Summons. The most important starter is "Windwitch - Ice Bell" which can Special Summon itself and another "Windwitch" monster from the deck while there are no monsters the user's field. The most important choice is to summon (though while also a potent starter itself) is the Level 4 Tuner monster "Windwitch - Glass Bell" which can search another "Windwitch" monster upon its summon. The ideal search choices are either "Windwi

main: Elzette, Azamina of the White Forest

Charmer.name: Charmer
Charmer.playing_style: "Unpossessed" is the key card of the Deck, preventing "Charmer" monsters from being destroyed by battle, which allows their Flip effects to be used properly, and Special Summoning Spellcaster monsters with 1500 DEF from the Deck, including non-members like "Injection Fairy Lily" or "Windwitch - Glass Bell".

main: Awakening of the Possessed - Nefariouser Archfiend
Awakening of the Possessed - Greater Inari Fire

World Chalice.name: World Chalice
World Chalice.playing_style: Being one of the first archetypes to focus on Link Summoning, the "World Chalice" archetype explores the intricacies of the newly-introduced mechanic. To that end, the archetype focuses on initiating multiple Link Summons in quick succession, all while promoting combos that extend the longevity of this style of play, namely through helping the controller amass more monsters in the hand or on the field to use as Link Materials. The archetype also encourages the controller carefully consider the p

Number 41: Bagooska the Terribly Tired T.name: Number 41: Bagooska the Terribly Tired Tapir
